description: Canonical pytest corpus for CI (SECP test-pipeline acceleration). The complete required backend test corpus, divided into balanced parallel shards. Every Git-tracked pytest-discoverable file under `roots` is assigned to exactly one shard; a new managed test file is auto-included, and a new pytest-shaped file outside `roots` fails the inventory closed unless it is listed in `exclusions`.

exclusions.reason: Runtime worker preflight-wiring self-test MODULE (SECP-B2-4.2), not a pytest suite. It matches the `*_test.py` glob but lives in the `secp_worker` source package (not a test root), contains no `test_*` functions or `Test*` classes, performs no I/O, and is imported as a normal module by apps/api/tests/test_readonly_preflight_activation.py (which exercises it as a unit under test). Collecting it as tests would collect zero nodes and misrepresent it as a suite.

Runtime worker readiness SELF-TEST seam (SECP-002B-1B B1B-PR4), not a pytest suite. It matches the `*_test.py` glob but lives in the `secp_worker` source package (not a test root), contains no `test_*` functions or `Test*` classes, performs no I/O (the shipped default is `SealedPlanSecretSelfTest`, which contacts nothing), and is imported as a normal module by the readiness orchestration and by apps/api/tests/test_readiness_*.py (which exercise it as a unit under test). Collecting it as tests would collect zero nodes and misrepresent it as a suite.

notes: roots is the authoritative corpus. It intentionally ADDS contracts/scenario-schema/tests, which the pre-acceleration workflow (`pytest apps/api/tests tests`) omitted — a coverage correction, not a scope expansion.
No pytest suites exist under apps/worker, contracts/plugin-api, plugins, or other packages (verified against the Git-tracked file inventory).

The PR5F fixed-layout root transaction module lives under the canonical `tests` root. Its dedicated Linux-root no-skip job is additive; normal shards still collect it and observe its explicit non-root skip.
Sharding is by FILE: every node in a file runs in the same shard, so the node-level union of shards equals the canonical collection with empty pairwise intersection.
PostgreSQL-gated, migration, immutability, concurrency, boundary, schema, and security tests are all inside roots and are never excluded; each shard runs against its own real PostgreSQL service.
